2603.23198.title: Sparser, Faster, Lighter Transformer Language Models
2603.23198.paper_summary: Scaling autoregressive large language models (LLMs) has driven unprecedented progress but comes with vast computational costs. In this work, we tackle these costs by leveraging unstructured sparsity within an LLM's feedforward layers, the components accounting for most of the model parameters and execution FLOPs. To achieve this, we introduce a new sparse packing format and a set of CUDA kernels designed to seamlessly integrate with the optimized execution pipelines of modern GPUs, enabling efficient sparse computation during LLM inference and training. To substantiate our gains, we provide a quantitative study of LLM sparsity, demonstrating that simple L1 regularization can induce over 99% sparsity with negligible impact on downstream performance. When paired with our kernels, we show that these sparsity levels translate into substantial throughput, energy efficiency, and memory usage benefits that increase with model scale. We will release all code and kernels under an open-source license to promote adoption and accelerate research toward establishing sparsity as a practical axis for improving the efficiency and scalability of modern foundation models.
2603.23198.paper_summary_zh: 擴展自回歸大型語言模型（LLMs）帶來了前所未有的進展，但也伴隨著龐大的計算成本。<lb/>在本研究中，我們通過利用LLM的前饋層中的非結構性稀疏性來解決這些成本，這些層是模型參數和執行FLOPs的大部分組成部分。<lb/>為了實現這一點，我們引入了一種新的稀疏打包格式和一組設計用於無縫集成現代GPU優化執行管道的CUDA內核，從而在LLM推理和訓練過程中實現高效的稀疏計算。<lb/>為了證實我們的收益，我們提供了一項LLM稀疏性的定量研究，表明簡單的L1正則化可以在對下游性能影響微乎其微的情況下引入超過99%的稀疏性。<lb/>當與我們的內核配合使用時，我們顯示這些稀疏水平轉化為可觀的吞吐量、能源效率和隨著模型規模增大而增加的內存使用效益。<lb/>我們將在開源許可下釋放所有代碼和內核，以促進採用並加速研究，旨在將稀疏性建立為提高現代基礎模型效率和可擴展性的實用軸心。
2603.23198.author: Edoardo Cetin et.al.
2603.23198.authors: Edoardo Cetin, Stefano Peluchetti, Emilio Castillo, Akira Naruse, Mana Murakami, Llion Jones
